system.name: wildflour-cafe
system.summary: Static website hosted on AWS Amplify and built from a GitHub repository on every push to `main` via a GitHub Actions deploy workflow. Bootstrapped from the Product Sweet website-bootstrap template; refine this summary once the project takes shape.
system.technology: Astro 5, AWS Amplify Hosting, GitHub Actions

relationships.label: stores source; PR + push events trigger the deploy workflow
runs lint + tests + build on PR; runs the deploy workflow on `main` push and triggers an Amplify RELEASE job
serves the built site; auto-build on `main` is disabled — releases are triggered exclusively by the deploy workflow
serves rendered HTML, CSS, JS, and assets to readers' browsers

externalSystems.name: GitHub
externalSystems.summary: Source repository hosting at https://github.com/productsweet-bootstrap-disposable/wildflour-cafe. Stores the framework source, the `.kanban/` task tree, the `.agent/` knowledge folder, and the GitHub Actions workflows committed by the bootstrap.

externalSystems.name: GitHub Actions
externalSystems.summary: CI/CD runner federated into AWS via OIDC (`token.actions.githubusercontent.com`). Runs the always-on `pr-gate.yml` — the single required status check — on every PR (it classifies the diff into a fast content lane or a full build lane in-job, so the required check reports on every PR including content-only ones), and `deploy-low.yml` on every `main` push (lint + tests + build, then triggers an Amplify RELEASE job to the low environment and waits on the result). Higher environments are promoted by `promote-<env>.yml` workflows — `workflow_dispatch`-only, no push trigger — dispatched by the Product Sweet webapp with the reviewed commit pinned as a `sha` input. Trust pin scopes to this repository's owner.

externalSystems.name: AWS Amplify Hosting
externalSystems.summary: Hosts the built site on the Amplify CDN. Auto-build on `main` is disabled; the bootstrap-installed deploy workflow is the only release path. Content-only changes (paths under `.kanban/` and `.agent/`) skip the rebuild via the env-cache rule.

externalSystems.name: Site Viewer
externalSystems.summary: End user reading the static site in a browser. The viewer's only relationship with this system is to fetch HTML, CSS, JS, and assets from Amplify Hosting.

containers.name: Static Site
containers.summary: The built static site itself — HTML, CSS, JS, and assets produced by the framework build and deployed to AWS Amplify Hosting on every `main` push. Per-framework overlays append framework-specific notes to the `technology` and `responsibilities` fields.
containers.technology: Astro static output — HTML emitted at build time, with optional component islands hydrated client-side
responsibilities: Render the project's content as static HTML at build time
Serve from AWS Amplify Hosting at https://main.det2fdp7wukhm.amplifyapp.com
Hydrate selectively — Astro components ship zero JS by default; islands opt in via `client:*` directives

containers.name: Build Pipeline
containers.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs lint + tests + framework build on every push to `main`, then triggers the Amplify RELEASE job and waits on its outcome. Same gates run on PRs without the release step.
containers.technology: GitHub Actions, Astro build (`astro build` → `dist/`), AWS CLI (Amplify StartJob)
responsibilities: Deploy `main` to AWS Amplify on push (lint + tests + build, then Amplify RELEASE)
Skip the rebuild for content-only changes under `.kanban/` and `.agent/`

relationships.label: triggers and awaits Amplify RELEASE jobs
